title: Cost substrate: CostMeter price table, ChatResponse.costUsd, Budget maxUsd
description: Build the money layer with no loop changes. A new pure CostMeter maps (provider, model, tokenUsage) to a USD estimate via a static all-provider price table (longest-prefix match, unknown → undefined). Every adapter populates optional ChatResponse.costUsd — claude-code returns its real, currently-discarded total_cost_usd and never estimates (ADR-3); anthropic/openai/openai-compat (and google where usage is normalized) use the CostMeter. Budget gains an optional maxUsd ceiling alongside tokens/agents.

successCriteria.description: estimateCostUsd({provider, model, usage}) returns the correct USD for known models via longest-prefix match on `${provider}:${modelPrefix}` keys and undefined for unknown models; the table carries rows for anthropic, openai, openai-compat (DeepSeek, xAI/Grok) and google with an as-priced date comment.
The claude-code adapter returns costUsd equal to the CLI's parsed total_cost_usd (vendor-authoritative), never a CostMeter estimate, and omits the field when the CLI output lacks it.
The anthropic and openai/openai-compat adapters set costUsd from estimateCostUsd when a price row matches and omit the field entirely when it returns undefined.

Budget accepts maxUsd (null/omitted = unlimited): chargeUsd accumulates into usdSpent; remainingUsd() returns Infinity when uncapped; exceeded() and assertWithinBudget() honor the USD axis with BudgetExceededError kind 'usd'; NaN/negative/undefined charges are treated as 0.
All pre-existing Budget token/agent tests and provider adapter tests pass unchanged (byte-identical when maxUsd/costUsd are absent).
npm run build and npm run typecheck pass.

nonGoals: Do not wire Budget into the agentic loop or any role (sprint 3).
Do not add config schema fields (sprint 3).
Do not make the price table config-overridable (explicitly declined in planning Q1).
Do not fetch prices at runtime — the table is static and in-repo.
stopConditions: npm run build passes and tests assert: prefix matching + undefined for unknown, claude-code real-cost passthrough, adapter estimate-or-omit behavior, and the full USD Budget axis including error kind 'usd'.
definitionOfDone: A pure src/providers/cost-meter.ts exposes estimateCostUsd with a dated all-provider PriceTable; every API adapter populates optional ChatResponse.costUsd (real for claude-code); Budget enforces an optional maxUsd with chargeUsd/usdSpent/remainingUsd and BudgetExceededError kind 'usd'. Nothing consumes these yet; omitting them is byte-identical. Build and full suite green.
assumptions: claude-code.ts already parses total_cost_usd (~:55) and discards it in the return block (~:182-190); this sprint threads it into the return.
ChatResponse (src/providers/types.ts:212-224) gains optional costUsd — LLMClient.chat signature unchanged.
Budget lives at src/orchestrator/workflow/budget.ts (BudgetOptions ~:19-24, BudgetExceededError ~:27-36); its existing workflow-interpreter callers must remain source-compatible.

ProviderName union comes from src/providers/factory.ts (~:13); DeepSeek/Grok run through openai-compat so their price keys use model-prefix distinctions (e.g. 'openai-compat:deepseek', 'openai-compat:grok').
Price values are best-known list prices at build time with an as-priced date comment; guardrail-not-billing semantics (approved planning answer Q1).
generatorNotes: Create src/providers/cost-meter.ts: export type PriceRow { inputPerMillion: number; outputPerMillion: number }, type PriceTable = Record<string, PriceRow> keyed `${provider}:${modelPrefix}`, and estimateCostUsd(input: {provider, model, usage}): number | undefined implementing longest-prefix match (sort matching keys by prefix length desc). Include a '// Prices as of 2026-07 (list prices; guardrail semantics, not billing)' comment. Populate rows for anthropic (claude-fable-5, claude-opus-4, claude-sonnet-5, claude-haiku-4-5 prefixes), openai, openai-compat:deepseek, openai-compat:grok, google:gemini — verify current list prices before hardcoding; if a price cannot be confirmed, omit the row (undefined is the safe fail-open) rather than guessing. Return undefined for provider 'claude-code' unconditionally. Extend budget.ts additively: maxUsd on BudgetOptions, chargeUsd (guard !Number.isFinite(usd) || usd < 0 → 0), usdSpent getter, remainingUsd() (Infinity when uncapped), extend exceeded()/assertWithinBudget(), add 'usd' to the kind union. In each adapter, compute costUsd right where the normalized ChatResponse is assembled (anthropic ~:348 return, openai usage normalize ~:455-457, openai-compat mirrors openai, claude-code return block ~:182-190) using conditional spread so the key is absent when undefined. Do NOT import cost-meter in claude-code.ts.
evaluatorNotes: Test estimateCostUsd: exact prefix, longer-prefix-wins (e.g. 'anthropic:claude-sonnet' vs 'anthropic:claude'), unknown model → undefined, claude-code → undefined, arithmetic correctness (tokens/1e6 * price summed for input+output) (sc-2-1). Mock the claude CLI JSON with and without total_cost_usd and assert passthrough/omission (sc-2-2). For adapters, stub the SDK response and deep-assert the returned ChatResponse: costUsd present with expected value for a priced model, key ABSENT (Object.hasOwn false) for an unpriced one (sc-2-3). Budget: charge sequences crossing the ceiling, remainingUsd Infinity uncapped, assertWithinBudget throws BudgetExceededError with kind 'usd', NaN/negative → 0 (sc-2-4). Run the FULL suite for sc-2-5 and build/typecheck for sc-2-6.
